text: Link Prediction based on Multi-Hyperplane Networks In this paper, we investigate the embedding problem of large-scale knowledge graph. As one of the most famous models, TransE achieved the highest prediction accuracy and efficiency. However, the relational mapping properties such as reflexivity, one-to-many, many-to-one and many-to-many are hard to be preserved by complex models due to the strict constraint of model complexity. In this paper, we propose a new model TransH, which models the relations as the translation on a specific hyperplane, thus preserves the complex mapping properties and keeps the same model complexity with TransE. To alleviate the influence of false negative samples generated by incomplete knowledge graph to the training process, we propose a novel method for generating negative samples based on the mapping properties of the relations. Experiment results on link prediction, triplet classification and fact extraction tasks from WordNet and Freebase show that TransH achieves higher prediction accuracy than the state-of-the-art method TransE while keeps the same high efficiency.
headline: Fully Human Written
